workflow.name: Route measurement data from a webhook to Google Sheets, email, or custom JS
workflow.description:
## Description

This workflow helps developers and automation teams **route measurement data from a webhook to multiple destinations** using n8n.

It is designed for **measurement engines, 3D scanning systems, QA tools, and API-based platforms** that send structured JSON data and need a fast, flexible way to store, display, or process it without building a custom backend.

The workflow starts with a **Webhook Trigger** that receives incoming measurement data and validates the payload. A processing step normalizes the fields into a clean, consistent format so the data can be reused across different outputs. A routing node then allows users to choose how they want to handle the data.

You can **append measurements to Google Sheets** for reporting, **display them as a formatted HTML page** for quick viewing, **export them as a CSV and send them by email**, or **run custom JavaScript logic** to forward the data to APIs, cloud storage, or other n8n workflows.

Clear sticky notes inside the workflow guide users through setup, credential configuration, and customization, making this template **beginner-friendly and production-ready**.

## How it works

1. A **Webhook Trigger** receives measurement data as a JSON payload.  
2. The workflow validates and normalizes the data structure.  
3. A **Switch node** routes the data to the selected output path.  
4. The data is sent to Google Sheets, an HTML response, a CSV email, or a custom JavaScript step.  

## How to set up

- Import and activate the workflow in n8n.  
- Copy the **Webhook URL** from the Webhook Trigger node.  
- Open the **3D Measure Up Web Application**:  
  https://3dmeasureup.ai/  
- Log in and navigate to **Settings → Webhooks**.  
- Paste the webhook URL into the **Webhook URL** field and click **Save**.  
- Connect Google Sheets and email credentials if needed.  
- Use the configuration fields and sticky notes inside the workflow to select and customize your output path.  

Setup usually takes **5–10 minutes**.

## Requirements

- n8n (cloud or self-hosted)  
- A webhook URL generated by this workflow  
- **3D Measure Up** can send measurement data as JSON to the webhook URL  
- Google account (optional, for Google Sheets output)  
- Email credentials (optional, for CSV email delivery)  

## How to update the Webhook URL in 3D Measure Up

1. Open the **3D Measure Up Web Application**:  
   https://3dmeasureup.ai/  
2. Log in to your account.  
3. Navigate to **Settings → Webhooks**.  
4. Copy the **Webhook Trigger URL** from this n8n workflow.  
5. Paste the URL into the **Webhook URL** field in 3D Measure Up.  
6. Click **Save** to apply the configuration.  

Once saved, the webhook becomes active immediately.  
From now on, whenever new measurements are generated, the data will be sent automatically to your n8n workflow.

## How to customize the workflow

- Adjust the **processing node** to match your measurement schema.  
- Enable or disable outputs using the **Switch node**.  
- Edit the HTML template for branding or layout.  
- Add API calls or storage logic inside the **JavaScript node**.

workflow.name: Route measurement data from Webhook to Google Sheets, Email or JS & 700+ apps

parameters.content:
## Webhook Entry Point

Receives POST requests with measurement data at:
`/measurement-data`

**Expected payload:**
- measurementId (required)
- timestamp (required)
- values (required)
- deviceId, operator, partNumber (optional)

##  Validation & Error Handling

**Checks for required fields:**
- measurementId
- timestamp
- values
































**If validation fails:**
- Returns 400 error response
- Stops workflow execution

## Data Processing

**Parse and Normalize:**
Extracts and structures measurement data including dimensions (x, y, z), tolerance, status, and metadata.

**Success Response:**
Returns 200 OK with confirmation message and measurementId.

## Output Routing

Routes data based on `outputMode` configuration:

- **sheets** → Google Sheets
- **html** → HTML Page
- **csv** → CSV Email
- **custom** → Custom JavaScript



















## Set outputMode in Workflow Configuration node.

## Google Sheets Output

Appends/updates measurement data to Google Sheets.

















⚠️ **Setup Required:**
- Configure Google Sheets credentials
- Set googleSheetId in Workflow Configuration
- Sheet name: "Measurements"

## 📄 HTML Report Output

Generates formatted HTML table with measurement data and returns as web page.
















## Useful for viewing data directly in browser.

## CSV Email Output

Converts data to CSV file and sends via email.

⚠️ **Setup Required:**
- Configure SMTP credentials
- Set emailRecipient in Workflow Configuration
- Customize emailSubject if needed

## Custom JavaScript Logic

Extensible code node with examples for:
- External API calls
- S3/cloud storage uploads
- Triggering other workflows
- Custom calculations
- Data transformations


















## Modify the code to fit your needs!

## Workflow Configuration

**Key Settings:**
- outputMode: 'sheets' | 'html' | 'csv' | 'custom'
- googleSheetId: Your Google Sheet ID
- emailRecipient: Email address for CSV
- emailSubject: Email subject line

Modify these values to customize behavior.

parameters.html:
Measurement Data Report

Dimension Z {{ $json.dimension_z }}
Tolerance {{ $json.tolerance }}
Status {{ $json.status }}
Notes {{ $json.notes }}

parameters.subject: ={{ $('Workflow Configuration').item.json.emailSubject }}

parameters.content:
# Measurement Data Routing Workflow

This workflow receives measurement data via webhook and routes it to multiple output destinations based on configuration.

## How it works:
1. **Webhook receives** POST requests with measurement data (ID, timestamp, dimensions, tolerance, metadata)
2. **Validation** ensures required fields are present; returns error if missing
3. **Data normalization** extracts and structures all measurement fields with defaults for optional values
4. **Success response** confirms receipt immediately (200 OK)
5. **Routing** directs data to one of four outputs based on the outputMode setting:
   - **sheets**: Appends to Google Sheets for tracking and analysis
   - **html**: Returns formatted HTML table for browser viewing
   - **csv**: Emails CSV file attachment to specified recipient
   - **custom**: Runs JavaScript code for API calls, cloud storage, or custom logic

## Setup steps:
1. **Configure output mode**: Edit "Workflow Configuration" node and set outputMode to your preferred destination
2. **Google Sheets** (if using sheets mode): Add Google Sheets credentials and update googleSheetId with your sheet ID
3. **Email** (if using csv mode): Add SMTP credentials and set emailRecipient to the destination address
4. **Custom code** (if using custom mode): Modify the JavaScript node with your integration logic
5. **Test**: Send a POST request to the webhook URL with sample measurement data



## How to get the Webhook URL:
- **Double click** on the 1st node **Webhook Trigger - Receive Measurement Data**
- You can see Test / Production URL section at the top in drop down ⌄ Webhook URLs
**Ex.** Webhook URL format: https://{your-cloud-name}.app.n8n.cloud/webhook/measurement-data


## How to take measurements:
- Import and activate the workflow in n8n.  
- Copy the **Webhook URL** from the Webhook Trigger node.  
- Open the **3D Measure Up Web Application**:   https://3dmeasureup.ai/  
- Log in and navigate to **Settings → Webhooks**.  
- Paste the webhook URL into the **Webhook URL** field and click **Save**. 

### Now, whenever you take a measurement with 3D Measure Up, it will be available in your n8n workflow

parameters.errorMessage: Invalid payload: Missing required fields (measurementId, timestamp, values)

assignments.name: googleSheetId
assignments.value: YOUR_SHEET_ID_HERE - Please replace with your actual Google Sheet ID

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
